messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
The ants move and bounce according to the given rules. Simulating the collisions and fall-offs:

- At \(t = 7.5\) s, ants A and B collide at 32.5 cm. Both reverse direction (A: \(-3\) cm/s, B: \(+1\) cm/s). Ant C continues left at \(-2\) cm/s.
- At \(t = \frac{55}{3}\) s, ant A falls off the left end, and simultaneously ants B and C collide at \(\frac{130}{3}\) cm. They reverse direction (B: \(-1\) cm/s, C: \(+2\) cm/s).
- Ant C falls off the right end at \(t = \frac{140}{3}\) s.
- Ant B falls off the left end at \(t = \frac{185}{3}\) s.

The last ant falls off at \(\frac{185}{3}\) seconds.

ANSWER: 185/3
